mnemonics: Divide and Conquer is like a Chef
Merge Sort is like Mixing Ingredients
Quick Sort is like Quickly Sorting the Dish
Binary Tree Traversals are like Brain Teasers
Multiplication of Large Integers is like Many Numbers to Multiply
Strassen's Matrix Multiplication is like Striking a Matrix Deal
keyPoints: Divide and Conquer is an algorithm design paradigm where problems are broken down into smaller sub-problems and then solved recursively.
Merge Sort uses the divide-and-conquer approach to sort an array by dividing it into two halves, sorting each half, and then merging them back together.
Quick Sort uses the divide-and-conquer approach to sort an array by choosing a pivot element, partitioning the array around it, and then recursively sorting the sub-arrays.
Binary Tree Traversals are used to visit each node in a binary tree exactly once, using three types of traversals: Inorder, Preorder, and Postorder.
Multiplication of Large Integers can be done using the standard multiplication algorithm, but it can be optimized using techniques such as the Karatsuba algorithm or the Monte Carlo method.
Strassen's Matrix Multiplication is an algorithm for matrix multiplication that uses the divide-and-conquer approach to reduce the time complexity from O(n^3) to O(n^2.81).
